question_text: At a meetup with unfamiliar attendees, the agent is asked to introduce themself to the room.
option_a: I step forward, greet everyone, and spark a brief group icebreaker.
option_b: I keep my introduction minimal and avoid initiating further conversation.

question_text: Networking event with optional speed-chat tables.
option_a: I sign up for multiple rounds and exchange contacts enthusiastically.
option_b: I skip the tables and leave early to avoid small talk.

question_text: I arrive early for a conference and several attendees are gathered by the coffee station.
option_a: I introduce myself, ask what sessions interest them, and keep the conversation moving.
option_b: I collect my drink and wait alone without approaching anyone.

question_text: A remote team meeting begins with a few minutes of silence before the agenda.
option_a: I leave my camera off and wait for someone else to speak.
option_b: I greet the group and start a brief check-in while everyone joins.

question_text: I attend a birthday gathering where the host is the only person I know.
option_a: I ask the host to introduce me, then start conversations with several guests.
option_b: I stay close to the host and avoid conversations with unfamiliar guests.

question_text: A cooperative game needs someone to explain the rules and organize the first round.
option_a: I remain at the edge of the group and let another player organize it.
option_b: I volunteer, explain the rules aloud, and invite everyone into the first round.

question_text: My team asks for a volunteer to demonstrate a new prototype to visiting colleagues.
option_a: I offer to run the demonstration and welcome questions from the visitors.
option_b: I avoid eye contact and hope someone else handles the demonstration.

question_text: A professional contact sends a friendly message after our first short meeting.
option_a: I give a minimal acknowledgment and do not continue the exchange.
option_b: I reply warmly, suggest a follow-up conversation, and introduce a useful shared topic.

question_text: Conversation pauses during a group dinner with people from different departments.
option_a: I offer a new topic and draw quieter people into the discussion.
option_b: I focus on my meal and wait silently for the dinner to end.

question_text: An online study cohort opens a channel for members to meet before the course starts.
option_a: I read the introductions without posting or responding.
option_b: I post an introduction, welcome new members, and start a discussion thread.

question_text: A neighborhood festival booth needs someone to greet visitors and explain its activity.
option_a: I take the greeting role and actively invite passersby to participate.
option_b: I choose a task behind the booth that requires little interaction.

question_text: Several people in a waiting area mention that the service is running late.
option_a: I keep to myself and avoid joining their conversation.
option_b: I respond with a friendly comment and chat with them while we wait.

question_text: A team celebration reaches the moment when someone could offer a short toast.
option_a: I stand up, share an upbeat toast, and invite the group to celebrate together.
option_b: I avoid attention and remain quiet even when others look for a speaker.

question_text: A new employee is assigned to sit near me during their first morning.
option_a: I work silently and wait for them to ask someone else for help.
option_b: I introduce myself, show them around, and connect them with nearby teammates.

question_text: A brainstorming session invites people to share unfinished ideas without preparation.
option_a: I voice several possibilities and build aloud on other people's suggestions.
option_b: I withhold my ideas because I do not want to speak spontaneously.

question_text: I recognize a former classmate across a crowded alumni event.
option_a: I avoid crossing the room because restarting the connection feels awkward.
option_b: I approach them, reopen the conversation, and meet the people with them.

question_text: People at a shared workspace are deciding whether to eat lunch together.
option_a: I suggest a place, invite the nearby group, and coordinate when to leave.
option_b: I eat alone without mentioning my plans to anyone.

question_text: A community video call allows attendees to join either with or without participating.
option_a: I join muted and do not contribute even when discussion opens.
option_b: I turn on my camera, introduce myself, and contribute to the discussion.

question_text: An informal tour group has not chosen anyone to keep the group together.
option_a: I volunteer to coordinate, check in with everyone, and keep the group engaged.
option_b: I follow at a distance and avoid taking any visible role.

question_text: A friend brings two people I have never met to a casual afternoon activity.
option_a: I acknowledge them briefly and spend the activity apart from them.
option_b: I welcome them, learn about their interests, and include them in our conversation.
